name: Adare Pitch and Putt
content: I visited Adare with my roommate on a day trip where we picked out a bus to anywhere, build-your-own-adventure style. We wound up in Adare. Figuring we might as well try to play some golf while we were in a place so well known for its golf, we made our way to the main golf course and were immediately met with a rules sign stating that no jeans were allowed on the course. My roommate and I slowly looked down to realize that both of us were wearing bright blue jeans. Such are the joys of living with a sitcom writer. So much for that plan. My roommate then told me he found a place called the “Pigeon Putt” which I assumed was a bird-themed mini golf course. Upon arriving at the “Pigeon Putt,” I read the sign that said “Pitch and Putt” and realized that I cannot hear for shit. We still had a great time at the Pitch and Putt; if you ever find yourself in Adare and feel gatekept from the wider golf community due to your jeans-wearing attitude, I would recommend giving the Pitch and Putt a shot.
